intent: Build a simple Python task management library (tasklib). It is not a production system. Its purpose is to validate that the Crafted Dev Agent build pipeline can close a complete dependency chain — from documentation through scaffold to working code — with real merges at each step.
scope_statement:
Build a minimal Python task management library (tasklib) as defined in TRD-TASKLIB to validate the Crafted Dev Agent build pipeline's ability to close a complete dependency chain — from documentation through scaffold to working code — with real merges at each step. The deliverable is a five-layer dependency chain (docs → scaffold → task model → in-memory store → CLI) with zero external dependencies, comprising documentation PRs (README, ARCHITECTURE, API), a package scaffold under src/tasklib/, a Task dataclass with TaskStatus enumeration, an InMemoryTaskStore with add/get/list/complete/delete operations, and a CLI entry point supporting add/list/complete commands.

prd_plan.title: Documentation Foundation — README, ARCHITECTURE, and API Reference for tasklib
Package Scaffold — directory tree, __init__.py sentinels, and tasklib-scoped pyproject.toml
Task Model — Task dataclass and TaskStatus enumeration with validation and serialization
In-Memory Task Store — InMemoryTaskStore with full CRUD operations
CLI Entry Point — argparse-based add/list/complete commands
